urdu transliteration text:
Ghurbat Main Qatl Ho Gaya Mazloom Bay Watan
Marshia Translations | ISIJ of Toronto
english translation text: Imam Hussain (AS) has been massacred heartlessly, away from home

urdu transliteration text:
Main Bhai Bhai Kehti Thi Thi Lasha Behan Behan
Marshia Translations | ISIJ of Toronto
english translation text: I would say “brother,” and the body would say “sister.”

urdu transliteration text:
Teeron Pay Tha Rakha Hua, Lasha Hussain KaDooba Hua Lahoo Main, Tha Kurta Hussain Ka
Marshia Translations | ISIJ of Toronto
english translation text: The body of Hussain lay on a bed of arrows. The shirt of Hussain was drenched in his blood

urdu transliteration text:
Awaaz Thi Hussain Ki Behna Kafan KafanMain Bhai Bhai Kehti Thi Thi Lasha Behan Behan
Marshia Translations | ISIJ of Toronto
english translation text: The body was crying, “shroud me, shroud, me”. I kept crying "O my brother, O my brother", while the body cried "O my sister, O my sister"

urdu transliteration text:
Aayi Na Raas Doo Gardhi, Pardes Ki FizaKunba Shaheed Ho Gaya, Mera Ghar Ujjargaya
Marshia Translations | ISIJ of Toronto
english translation text: The strange environment did not suit even for a moment. My family was killed, we were destroyed

urdu transliteration text:
Pardes, Des Ghair Ka, Apna Watan WatanMain Bhai Bhai Kehti Thi Thi Lasha Behan Behan
Marshia Translations | ISIJ of Toronto
english translation text: Other’s land is strange, our home is homeI kept crying "O my brother, O my brother", while the body cried "O my sister, O my sister"

urdu transliteration text:
Ik Din Kay Dagh Mainay, Bahattar Utha LiyeKuch Dagh Mainay Qalb Kay, Andhar Chupa Liye
Marshia Translations | ISIJ of Toronto
english translation text: In just one day, I witnessed 72 losses I have concealed some of the sorrows inside my heart

urdu transliteration text:
Chuptay Nahin, Chupa Nay Say Dagh-e-Rasan RasanMain Bhai Bhai Kehti Thi Thi Lasha Behan Behan
Marshia Translations | ISIJ of Toronto
english translation text: However, I am unable to hide the marks of ropes on my body. I kept crying "O my brother, O my brother", while the body cried "O my sister, O my sister"

urdu transliteration text:
Dekha Hay Baba Mainay, Jo Manzar Bataoun KiyaNaizon Pay Tha Rakha Hua, Sar Har Shaheed Ka
Marshia Translations | ISIJ of Toronto
english translation text: O baba, how can I relay to you what all I have seen? The head of all martyrs were raised on spears

urdu transliteration text:
Ghoron Kay Dawr Nay Say Tha Tukray Badan BadanMain Bhai Bhai Kehti Thi Thi Lasha Behan
Marshia Translations | ISIJ of Toronto
english translation text: With the trampling of the houses, the bodies are in pieces. I kept crying "O my brother, O my brother", while the body cried "O my sister”

urdu transliteration text:
Qasim Ki Lash Laye, Thay Daman Main Shah-e-Deen Main Bhai Bhai Kehti Thi Lasha Behan Behan
Marshia Translations | ISIJ of Toronto
english translation text: Qasim’s body Husayn carried in his lap. I would say “brother,” and the body would say “sister.”

urdu transliteration text:
Baba Hassan Say Taaba Hussain-e-shaheed Tak
Baba Ameer-e-shaam Say, Daur-e-yazeed Tak
Marshia Translations | ISIJ of Toronto
english translation text: O father, from Hasan to Husain’s timeO father, from Mu‘awiya to Yazid’s time

urdu transliteration text:
Loota Hay Ahl-e-shaam Nay Baba Chaman Chaman
Main Bhai Bhai Kehti Thi Lasha Behan Behan
Marshia Translations | ISIJ of Toronto
english translation text: O father, the Syrians have destroyed our ‘garden’. I would say “brother,” and the body would say “sister.”

urdu transliteration text:
Barchi Kalayja-e-Ali Akbar Main Jab Lagi
Baba Ko Di Sada Kabhi Awaaz Mujhko Di
Marshia Translations | ISIJ of Toronto
english translation text: When the spearhead penetrated Akbar’s chest. He called his father, & sometimes called me

urdu transliteration text:
Kehta Tha Seena Tham Kay Amma Chubhan Chubhan
Main Bhai Bhai Kehti Thi Lasha Behan Behan
Marshia Translations | ISIJ of Toronto
english translation text: Hand on the chest, he cried: mother, its hurting. I would say “brother,” and the body would say “sister.”

urdu transliteration text:
Ghazi Kay Baad Aur Qayamat Guzargayi
Pysaason Ki Aas Toot Kay Ban Main Bikhargayi
Marshia Translations | ISIJ of Toronto
english translation text: After Abbas’ death, the sky fall throughShattering the hope of thirsty ones in the fields

urdu transliteration text:
Ik Shaur Al-Atash Ka Bapa Tha Dehan Dehan, Main Bhai Bhai Kehti Thi Thi Lasha Behan Behan
Marshia Translations | ISIJ of Toronto
english translation text: Every mouth was shouting al-‘atash, O thirst! O thirst! I would say “brother,” and the body would say “sister.”
